R. 8870\nIN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES\nMay 19, 2026 Mr. Graves (for himself, Mr. Larsen of Washington , Mr. Rouzer , Mr. Webster of Florida , and Ms. Norton ) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure\nA BILL\nTo authorize funding for Federal-aid highways, bridge construction and rehabilitation, highway safety programs, transit programs, and rail programs, and for other purposes.\n#### 1. Short title; table of contents\n##### (a) Short title\nThis Act may be cited as the Building Unrivaled Infrastructure and Long-term Development for America\u2019s 250th Act or the BUILD America 250 Definitions\nIn this Act:\n**(1) Comptroller General**\nThe term Comptroller General means the Comptroller General of the United States.\n**(2) Department**\nUnless otherwise specified, the term Department means the Department of Transportation.\n**(3) Secretary**\nUnless otherwise specified, the term Secretary means the Secretary of Transportation.\n#### 3. Effective date\nExcept as otherwise provided, this Act, including the amendments made by this Act, shall take effect on October 1, 2026.\n#### 4. Automatic execution of conforming changes\n##### (a) Covered highways laws\nSection 101 of title 23, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:\n(f) Automatic execution of conforming changes (1) In general When an amendment to a covered highways law adds a section or larger organizational unit to the covered highways law, repeals or transfers a section or larger organizational unit in the covered highways law, or amends the designation or heading of a section or larger organizational unit in the covered highways law, that amendment also shall have the effect of amending any analysis, table of contents, or similar tabular entries in the covered highways law to alter the table to conform to the changes made by the amendment. (2) Exceptions Paragraph (1) shall not apply to an amendment described in such paragraph when\u2014 (A) the amendment or a clerical amendment enacted at the same time expressly amends a table of sections, table of contents, or similar tabular entries in the covered highways law to alter the table to conform to the changes made by the amendment; or (B) the amendment otherwise expressly exempts itself from the operation of this subsection. (3) Covered highways law defined In this subsection, the term covered highways law means\u2014 (A) this title; (B) any Act that authorizes amounts to be appropriated out of the Highway Trust Fund; or (C) any other law designated in the text thereof as a covered highways law for purposes of application of this subsection. .\n##### (b) Purpose and automatic execution of conforming changes\nSection 101 of title 49, United States Code, is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin the section heading by inserting ; automatic execution of conforming changes after Purpose ; and\n**(2)**\nby adding at the end the following:\n(c) Automatic execution of conforming changes (1) In general When an amendment to a covered transportation law adds a section or larger organizational unit to the covered transportation law, repeals or transfers a section or larger organizational unit in the covered transportation law, or amends the designation or heading of a section or larger organizational unit in the covered transportation law, that amendment also shall have the effect of amending any analysis, table of contents, or similar tabular entries in the covered transportation law to alter the table to conform to the changes made by the amendment. (2) Exceptions Paragraph (1) shall not apply to an amendment described in such paragraph when\u2014 (A) the amendment or a clerical amendment enacted at the same time expressly amends a table of sections, table of contents, or similar tabular entries in the covered transportation law to alter the table to conform to the changes made by the amendment; or (B) the amendment otherwise expressly exempts itself from the operation of this subsection. (3) Covered transportation law In this subsection, the term covered transportation law means\u2014 (A) this title; (B) any Act that authorizes amounts to be appropriated out of the Airport and Airway Trust Fund; or (C) any other law designated in the text thereof as a covered transportation law for purposes of application of this subsection. .\n##### (c) Application of amendments\nSection 101(f) of title 23, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), and section 101(c) of title 49, United States Code, as added by subsection (b), shall apply to the amendments made by this section and other amendments made by this Act.\nI\nFederal-Aid Highways\nA\nAuthorizations and Programs\n#### 1101. Authorization of appropriations\n##### (a) In general\nThe following amounts are authorized to be appropriated out of the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account):\n**(1) Federal-aid highway program**\nFor the national highway performance program under section 119 of title 23, United States Code, the surface transportation block grant program under section 133 of such title, section 134 of such title, the highway safety improvement program under section 148 of such title, the congestion mitigation and air quality improvement program under section 149 of such title, and the national highway freight and highway priority corridor program of section 167 of such title\u2014\n**(A)**\n$56,934,650,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(B)**\n$57,532,010,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(C)**\n$58,690,676,200 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(D)**\n$59,785,644,724 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(E)**\n$60,943,911,618 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(2) Transportation infrastructure finance and innovation program**\nFor credit assistance under the transportation infrastructure finance and innovation program under chapter 6 of title 23, United States Code, $250,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(3) Bridge program**\nTo carry out the grants for rebuilding America\u2019s vital engineering structures program under section 124(a) of title 23, United States Code, $9,200,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(4) Federal lands and Tribal transportation programs**\n**(A) Tribal transportation program**\nFor the tribal transportation program under section 202 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$643,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$657,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$671,000,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$686,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$701,000,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(B) Federal lands transportation program**\n**(i) In general**\nFor the Federal lands transportation program under section 203 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(I)**\n$464,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(II)**\n$472,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(III)**\n$480,000,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(IV)**\n$488,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(V)**\n$496,000,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(ii) Allocation**\nOf the amount made available for a fiscal year under clause (i)\u2014\n**(I)**\nthe amount for the National Park Service is\u2014\n**(aa)**\n$365,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(bb)**\n$370,500,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(cc)**\n$376,000,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(dd)**\n$381,500,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(ee)**\n$387,500,000 for fiscal year 2031;\n**(II)**\nthe amount for the United States Fish and Wildlife Service is $42,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031; and\n**(III)**\nthe amount for the Forest Service is\u2014\n**(aa)**\n$29,500,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(bb)**\n$31,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(cc)**\n$32,500,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(dd)**\n$34,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(ee)**\n$35,500,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(C) Federal lands access program**\nFor the Federal lands access program under section 204 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$314,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$320,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$326,000,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$332,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$338,000,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(5) Territorial and Puerto Rico highway program**\nFor the territorial and Puerto Rico highway program under section 165 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(A)**\n$242,200,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(B)**\n$247,400,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(C)**\n$252,600,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(D)**\n$257,800,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(E)**\n$263,000,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n##### (b) Other programs\n**(1) Highway Trust Fund**\nThe following amounts are authorized to be appropriated out of the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account):\n**(A) Safe streets and roads for all program**\nTo carry out the safe streets and roads for all program under section 155 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$500,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$625,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$750,000,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$875,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$1,000,000,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(B) Surface transportation accelerator grant program**\nTo carry out the surface transportation accelerator grant program under section 173 of title 23, United States Code, $2,400,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(C) PROTECT grants**\nTo carry out subsection (d) of the PROTECT program under section 176 of title 23, United States Code, $500,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(D) Nationally significant Federal lands and Tribal projects**\n**(i) In general**\nTo carry out the nationally significant Federal lands and tribal projects program under section 1123 of the FAST Act ( 23 U.S.C. 201 note; Public Law 114\u201394 ), $55,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(ii) Treatment**\nAmounts made available under clause (i) shall be available for obligation in the same manner as if those amounts were apportioned under chapter 1 of title 23, United States Code.\n**(2) General fund**\n**(A) Nationally significant multimodal freight and highway projects**\nThere is authorized to be appropriated to carry out the nationally significant multimodal freight and highway projects program under section 117 of title 23, United States Code, $1,200,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(B) Bridge completion program**\nThere is authorized to be appropriated to carry out the bridge completion program under section 124(b) of title 23, United States Code, $2,000,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n##### (c) Research, technology, and education authorizations\n**(1) In general**\nThe following amounts are authorized to be appropriated out of the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account):\n**(A) Highway research and development program**\nTo carry out section 503(b) of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$149,940,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$152,938,800 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$155,997,576 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$159,117,528 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$162,299,878 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(B) Technology and innovation deployment**\nTo carry out section 503(c) of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$112,200,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$114,444,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$116,732,880 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$119,067,538 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$121,448,888 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(C) Training and education**\nTo carry out section 504 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$26,520,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$27,050,400 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$27,591,408 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$28,143,236 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$28,706,101 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(D) Intelligent transportation systems program**\nTo carry out sections 512 through 518 of title 23, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$112,200,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$114,444,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$116,732,880 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$119,067,538 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$121,448,888 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(E) University transportation centers program**\nTo carry out section 5505 of title 49, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$83,640,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$85,312,800 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$87,019,056 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$88,759,437 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$90,534,626 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(F) Bureau of transportation statistics**\nTo carry out chapter 63 of title 49, United States Code\u2014\n**(i)**\n$27,250,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(ii)**\n$27,500,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(iii)**\n$27,750,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(iv)**\n$28,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(v)**\n$28,250,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n**(2) Administration**\nThe Administrator of the Federal Highway Administration shall\u2014\n**(A)**\nadminister the programs described in subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) under paragraph (1); and\n**(B)**\nin consultation with relevant modal administrations, administer the programs described in paragraph (1)(D).\n**(3) Applicability of title 23, United States Code**\nAmounts authorized to be appropriated by paragraph (1) shall\u2014\n**(A)**\nbe available for obligation in the same manner as if those funds were apportioned under chapter 1 of title 23, United States Code, except that the Federal share of the project or activity carried out using those funds shall be 80 percent, unless otherwise expressly provided by this Act (including the amendments made by this Act) or otherwise determined by the Secretary; and\n**(B)**\nremain available until expended and are not transferable, except as otherwise provided by this Act.\n##### (d) Pilot programs\nThe following amounts are authorized to be appropriated out of the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account):\n**(1) Wildlife crossings**\nFor the wildlife crossings pilot program under section 171 of title 23, United States Code, $80,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n**(2) Truck parking**\nFor the truck parking pilot program under section 180 of title 23, United States Code, $150,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.\n##### (e) Disadvantaged business enterprises\n**(1) Findings**\nCongress finds that\u2014\n**(A)**\nwhile significant progress has occurred due to the establishment of the disadvantaged business enterprise program, social and economic disadvantage and related barriers continue to pose significant obstacles for businesses owned by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals seeking to do business in federally assisted surface transportation markets across the United States;\n**(B)**\nthe continuing barriers described in subparagraph (A) merit the continuation of the disadvantaged business enterprise program;\n**(C)**\nCongress has received and reviewed documentation of the effects of social and economic disadvantage on individuals seeking to do business in federally assisted surface transportation markets from numerous sources, including congressional hearings and roundtables, scientific and other reports, news stories, written statements of barriers to participation by disadvantaged business owners, and related lawsuits, which show that efforts that fail to specifically consider socially and economically disadvantaged individuals are insufficient to address the problem;\n**(D)**\nthe documentation described in subparagraph (C) demonstrates that barriers remain for the full and fair participation in surface transportation-related businesses of socially and economically disadvantaged business owners and has impacted firm development and many aspects of surface transportation-related business in the public and private markets; and\n**(E)**\nthe documentation described in subparagraph (C) provides a strong basis that there is a compelling need for the continuation of the disadvantaged business enterprise program.\n**(2) Small business concern defined**\nIn this subsection:\n**(A) In general**\nThe term small business concern means a small business concern (as the term is used in section 3 of the Small Business Act ( 15 U.S.C. 632 )).\n**(B) Exclusions**\nThe term small business concern does not include any concern or group of concerns controlled by the same socially and economically disadvantaged individual or individuals that have average annual gross receipts during the preceding 3 fiscal years in excess of $31,840,000, as adjusted annually by the Secretary for inflation.\n**(3) Amounts for small business concerns**\nA national, aspirational goal of not less than 10 percent of the amounts made available for any program under titles I, II, III, and VI of this Act and section 403 of title 23, United States Code, shall be set for expenditure through good faith efforts by recipients of Federal financial assistance through small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.\n**(4) Development of objective criteria**\n**(A) In general**\nNot later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall develop and publish objective criteria to establish how State governments and unified certification programs will evaluate whether an individual qualifies as socially and economically disadvantaged under the program.\n**(B) Considerations**\nThe criteria developed under subparagraph (A)\u2014\n**(i)**\nshall include the ability for an individual to demonstrate social and economic disadvantage by submitting evidence that would support a finding of the types of discrimination prohibited under Federal law; and\n**(ii)**\nshall include the ability for an individual to submit evidence of specific instances of economic hardship, systemic barriers, and denied opportunities that impeded the individual from achieving educational progress or success, employment opportunities, or business opportunities (including access to capital).\n**(C) Periodic revision**\nThe Secretary may periodically revise the objective criteria developed under subparagraph (A).\n**(5) Annual listing of disadvantaged business enterprises**\nEach State shall annually\u2014\n**(A)**\nsurvey and compile a list of the small business concerns referred to in paragraph (3) in the State, including the location of the small business concerns in the State;\n**(B)**\nnotify the Secretary, in writing, of the number of new small business concerns that have been certified in the State in the previous year; and\n**(C)**\nprovide the Secretary with such other information as the Secretary may require regarding the administration of the disadvantaged business enterprise program.\n**(6) Uniform certification**\n**(A) In general**\nThe Secretary shall establish minimum uniform criteria for use by State governments in certifying whether a concern qualifies as a small business concern for the purpose of this subsection.\n**(B) Inclusions**\nThe minimum uniform criteria established under subparagraph (A) shall include, with respect to a potential small business concern\u2014\n**(i)**\non-site visits;\n**(ii)**\npersonal interviews with personnel;\n**(iii)**\nissuance or inspection of licenses;\n**(iv)**\nanalyses of stock ownership;\n**(v)**\nlistings of equipment;\n**(vi)**\nanalyses of bonding capacity;\n**(vii)**\nlistings of work completed;\n**(viii)**\nexamination of the resumes of principal owners;\n**(ix)**\nanalyses of financial capacity; and\n**(x)**\nanalyses of the type of work preferred.\n**(7) Reporting**\nThe Secretary shall establish minimum requirements for use by State governments in reporting to the Secretary\u2014\n**(A)**\ninformation concerning disadvantaged business enterprise awards, commitments, and achievements;\n**(B)**\nthe process utilized and progress made by each unified certification program in the State to evaluate and recertify or decertify a small business concern under the criteria set by the Secretary, including periodic revision of the criteria for certification;\n**(C)**\nthe number of existing small business concerns recertified or decertified in fiscal years 2026 through 2031; and\n**(D)**\nsuch other information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate for the proper monitoring of the disadvantaged business enterprise program.\n**(8) Compliance with court orders**\nNothing in this subsection limits the eligibility of an individual or entity to receive funds made available under titles I, II, III, and VI of this Act and section 403 of title 23, United States Code, if the entity or person is prevented, in whole or in part, from complying with paragraph (3) because a Federal court issues a final order in which the court finds that a requirement or the implementation of paragraph (3) is unconstitutional.\n**(9) Sense of Congress on prompt payment of DBE subcontractors**\nIt is the sense of Congress that\u2014\n**(A)**\nthe Secretary should take additional steps to ensure that recipients comply with section 26.29 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (the disadvantaged business enterprises prompt payment rule), or any corresponding regulation, in awarding federally funded transportation contracts under laws and regulations administered by the Secretary; and\n**(B)**\nsuch additional steps should include increasing the Department\u2019s ability to track and keep records of complaints and to make that information publicly available.\n##### (f) Grant conditions\nThe Secretary may not terminate, withhold, or delay the execution of a grant agreement for a grant or award (in part or in whole) made using funds made available under this Act (or an amendment made by this Act) on the basis that the grant or award no longer effectuates non-statutory program goals or agency priorities, including pursuant to section 200.340(a)(4) of title 2, Code of Federal Regulations.\n#### 1102. Obligation limitation\n##### (a) General limitation\nSubject to subsection (e) and notwithstanding any other provision of law, the obligations for the Federal-aid highway and highway safety construction programs shall not exceed\u2014\n**(1)**\n$72,270,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;\n**(2)**\n$73,045,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;\n**(3)**\n$74,382,000,000 for fiscal year 2029;\n**(4)**\n$75,657,000,000 for fiscal year 2030; and\n**(5)**\n$76,996,000,000 for fiscal year 2031.\n##### (b) Exceptions\nThe limitations under subsection (a) shall not apply to obligations under or for\u2014\n**(1)**\nsection 125 of title 23, United States Code;\n**(2)**\nsection 147 of the Surface Transportation Assistance Act of 1978 ( 23 U.S.C. 144 note; 92 Stat. 2714);\n**(3)**\nsection 9 of the Federal-Aid Highway Act of 1981 (95 Stat. 1701);\n**(4)**\nsubsections (b) and (j) of section 131 of the Surface Transportation Assistance Act of 1982 (96 Stat. 2119);\n**(5)**\nsubsections (b) and (c) of section 149 of the Surface Transportation and Uniform Relocation Assistance Act of 1987 (101 Stat. 198);\n**(6)**\nsections 1103 through 1108 of the Intermodal Surface Transportation Efficiency Act of 1991 (105 Stat. 2027);\n**(7)**\nsection 157 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect on June 8, 1998);\n**(8)**\nsection 105 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect for fiscal years 1998 through 2004, but only in an amount equal to $639,000,000);\n**(9)**\nFederal-aid highway programs for which obligation authority was made available under the Transportation Equity Act for the 21st Century (112 Stat. 107) or subsequent Acts for multiple years or to remain available until expended, but only to the extent that the obligation authority has not lapsed or been used;\n**(10)**\nsection 105 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect for fiscal years 2005 through 2012, but only in an amount equal to $639,000,000 for each of those fiscal years);\n**(11)**\nsection 1603 of the SAFETEA\u2013LU ( 23 U.S.C. 118 note), to the extent that funds obligated in accordance with such section were not subject to a limitation on obligations at the time at which the funds were initially made available for obligation;\n**(12)**\nsection 119 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect for fiscal years 2013 through 2015, but only in an amount equal to $639,000,000 for each of those fiscal years);\n**(13)**\nsection 119 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect for fiscal years 2016 through 2021, but only in an amount equal to $639,000,000 for each of those fiscal years);\n**(14)**\nsection 119 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect for fiscal years 2022 through 2026, but only in an amount equal to $639,000,000 for each of those fiscal years); and\n**(15)**\nsection 119 of title 23, United States Code (as in effect for fiscal years 2027 through 2031, but only in an amount equal to $639,000,000 for each of those fiscal years).\n##### (c) Distribution of obligation authority\nFor each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031, the Secretary\u2014\n**(1)**\nshall not distribute obligation authority provided by subsection (a) for the fiscal year for\u2014\n**(A)**\namounts authorized for administrative expenses and programs by section 104(a) of title 23, United States Code; and\n**(B)**\namounts authorized for the Bureau of Transportation Statistics;\n**(2)**\nshall not distribute an amount of obligation authority provided by subsection (a) that is equal to the unobligated balance of amounts\u2014\n**(A)**\nmade available from the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account) for the Federal-aid highway and highway safety construction programs for previous fiscal years the funds for which are allocated by the Secretary (or apportioned by the Secretary under section 202 or 204 of title 23, United States Code); and\n**(B)**\nfor which obligation authority was provided in a previous fiscal year;\n**(3)**\nshall determine the proportion that\u2014\n**(A)**\nthe obligation authority provided by subsection (a) for the fiscal year, less the aggregate of the amount not distributed under paragraphs (1) and (2) of this subsection; bears to\n**(B)**\nthe total of sums authorized to be appropriated for the Federal-aid highway and highway safety construction programs (other than sums authorized to be appropriated for provisions of law described in paragraphs (1) through (14) of subsection (b) and sums authorized to be appropriated for section 119 of title 23, United States Code, equal to the amount referred to in subsection (b)(15) for the fiscal year), less the aggregate of the amounts not distributed under paragraphs (1) and (2) of this subsection;\n**(4)**\nshall distribute the obligation authority provided by subsection (a), less the aggregate amounts not distributed under paragraphs (1) and (2), for each of the programs (other than programs to which paragraph (1) applies) that are allocated by the Secretary under this Act and title 23, United States Code, or apportioned by the Secretary under section 202 or 204 of such title, by multiplying\u2014\n**(A)**\nthe proportion determined under paragraph (3); by\n**(B)**\nthe amounts authorized to be appropriated for each such program for the fiscal year;\n**(5)**\nsubject to paragraph (6), shall distribute the obligation authority provided by subsection (a), less the aggregate amount not distributed under paragraphs (1) and (2) and the amounts distributed under paragraph (4), for the Federal-aid highway and highway safety construction programs that are apportioned by the Secretary under title 23, United States Code (other than the amounts apportioned for the national highway performance program in section 119 of title 23, United States Code, that are exempt from limitation under subsection (b)(15) and the amounts apportioned under sections 202 and 204 of such title) in the proportion that\u2014\n**(A)**\namounts authorized to be appropriated for the programs that are apportioned under title 23, United State Code, to each State for the fiscal year; bears to\n**(B)**\nthe total of the amounts authorized to be appropriated for the programs that are apportioned under title 23, United States Code, to all States for the fiscal year; and\n**(6)**\nof the amounts calculated for a State under paragraph (5), distribute to any direct recipient designated under section 1113(d) located in such State, or proportionally located in such State\u2014\n**(A)**\nfor a direct recipient located in 1 State, an amount of obligation authority described in section 1113(d)(4)(B); or\n**(B)**\nfor a direct recipient located in more than 1 State, a proportional amount of obligation authority described in section 1113(d)(4)(B).\n##### (d) Redistribution of unused obligation authority\nNotwithstanding subsection (c), the Secretary shall, after August 1 of each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031\u2014\n**(1)**\nrevise a distribution of the obligation authority made available under subsection (c) if an amount distributed cannot be obligated during the fiscal year; and\n**(2)**\nredistribute sufficient amounts to those States able to obligate amounts in addition to those previously distributed during the fiscal year, giving priority to those States having large unobligated balances of funds apportioned under section 104 of title 23, United States Code, and paragraph (1) under the heading Highway Infrastructure Program in title VIII of division J of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (135 Stat. 1420), commonly referred to as the Bridge Formula Program .\n##### (e) Applicability of obligation limitations to certain programs\n**(1) Transportation research programs**\n**(A) In general**\nExcept as provided in subparagraph (B), obligation limitations imposed by subsection (a) shall apply to contract authority for transportation research programs carried out under chapter 5 of title 23, United States Code.\n**(B) Exception**\nObligation authority made available under subparagraph (A) shall\u2014\n**(i)**\nremain available for a period of 4 fiscal years; and\n**(ii)**\nbe in addition to the amount of any limitation on obligations for the Federal-aid highway and highway safety construction programs for future fiscal years.\n**(2) Direct recipient metropolitan planning organizations**\n**(A) In general**\nExcept as provided in subparagraph (B), obligation limitations imposed by subsection (a) shall apply to contract authority apportioned under section 104(b)(6) of title 23, United States Code, that is directly allocated under section 1113(d)(4)(B) of this Act to a direct recipient designated under such section.\n**(B) Exception**\nObligation authority made available under subparagraph (A) shall\u2014\n**(i)**\nremain available for a period of 2 fiscal years; and\n**(ii)**\nbe in addition to the amount of any limitation imposed on obligations for Federal-aid highway and highway safety construction programs for future fiscal years.\n##### (f) Redistribution of certain authorized funds\n**(1) In general**\nNot later than 30 days after the date of distribution of obligation authority under subsection (c) for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031, the Secretary shall distribute to the States any funds (excluding funds authorized for the program under section 202 of title 23, United States Code) that\u2014\n**(A)**\nare authorized to be appropriated for the fiscal year for the Federal-aid highway programs; and\n**(B)**\nthe Secretary determines will not be allocated to the States (or will not be apportioned to the States under section 204 of title 23, United States Code), and will not be available for obligation, for the fiscal year because of the imposition of any obligation limitation for the fiscal year.\n**(2) Ratio**\nFunds shall be distributed under paragraph (1) in the same proportion as the distribution of obligation authority under subsection (c)(5).\n**(3) Availability**\nFunds distributed to each State under paragraph (1) shall be available for any purpose described in section 133(b) of title 23, United States Code.\n#### 1103. Definitions\nSection 101(a) of title 23, United States Code, is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin paragraph (18) by striking and traffic control centers and inserting , traffic control centers, and backup power systems for traffic control devices and systems ;\n**(2)**\nby redesignating paragraphs (6) through (36) as paragraphs (7) through (37), respectively; and\n**(3)**\nby inserting after paragraph (5) the following:\n(6) Digital infrastructure The term digital infrastructure means public and private technology assets, including advanced digital construction management systems and related technology, that create, exchange, secure, or use data, including communications systems, servers, routers, hardware, sensors, and software applications. .\n#### 1104.
